rulings.Case Title: BECK & BLOOM, LLC VS. HAWK LOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action (Lis Pendens) Or, In The Alternative, To Have Plaintiff Give An Undertaking; Claim For Attorneys Fees And Costs C.C.P,, Section 405.30, Et Seq
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 22, 2016 line 5 Defednant's Motion to expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action (Lis Pendens) Or, In The Alternative, To Have Plaintiff Give An Undertaking; Claim For Attorneys Fees And Costs C.C.P,, Section 405.30, Et Seq GRANTED. A real property claim is stated. Plaintiff failed to show by the preponderance of evidence the probable validity of this claim. No evidence is provided as to if and when the Premises will be available for intended occupancy, e.g. months, years, decades. No authority is provided for the proposition that the leasehold interest will continue after the expiration of the term (including extension, if applicable) of the lease. Defendants shall recover $2,940 in fees and costs. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHOO KOON NG VS. DAMIEN QUINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate And To Enter Judgment Against Defendants After Repossession Of The Premises
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 22, 2016 line 6. PLAINTIFF CHOO NG's Motion To Consolidate (653867 and 653869) And To Enter Judgment Against Defendants After Repossession Of The Premises GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to consolidate is granted. Cases shall be consolidated for all purposes and CUD-15-653867 shall be the lead case. Motion for Entry of Money Judgment is DENIED. Per Stipulation of file and CCP 664.6 this Court has no basis to enter a money judgment. The Stipulation is for Possession only in the event of breach. Since possession is no longer in issue these actions became ordinary civil actions by operation of law (CC 1952.3) and Plaintiff shall proceed accordingly. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES VS. ANDRES SUAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion To Dismiss With Exhibits

DEFENDANT ANN LEMBERG's Amended Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion To Dismiss With Exhibits DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CCP 1005 and carry its burden under CCP 1008. = (501/REQ)
